_description: Tracks whether Rob's attention is currently claimed by a specific session's decision request. Other sessions check this on startup to understand if Rob is mid-decision. If Rob is occupied, start with tasks that don't need immediate approval.
_usage: Write to this file when blocking on a Rob decision (warm lead, APPROVE SEND, etc.). Clear it when Rob responds. Other sessions read it during Phase 5 of startup.

current_request.context: Seth Drummond @ Northern Trust replied positively — should I prioritize warm lead response or continue T1 batch?
Batch 8 — 9 contacts ready for APPROVE SEND review. See tamob-batch-20260313-1.html

_guidance_for_sessions: If rob-availability.status is 'busy', pick a task that doesn't require immediate Rob input: T2 drafting, compliance gate checks, batch tracker HTML building, context window optimization work. Post an [INFO] message in messages.md: 'Session X online — noted Rob is working with Session Y on [type]. Starting Task-Z meanwhile.'
